interactions.summary: Jiang extends the front-person theory through HP, Stanford, Rockefeller/Carnegie, and charity, then answers science-as-religion by starting from COVID vaccine obedience.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

speaker_notes.note: Diarization is consistent enough for this packet: SPEAKER_00 carries host questions, intro, outro, or sponsor reads; SPEAKER_01 carries Jiang answers. Sponsor ad segments are not Jiang claims.

claims.claim: Jiang says the public billionaire-founder story is recent; earlier tycoons like Rockefeller and Carnegie functioned as agents of the City of London and used charity to disguise how much money they controlled.
Jiang argues COVID vaccine acceptance showed science functioning like religion because most people accepted authority rather than questioning logic, mutation, and evidence.

signature_moments.moment: The self-made billionaire becomes an agent wearing the American dream as disguise.
signature_moments.source_phrase: agents, representatives of the city of London
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It reverses the heroic founder myth.

signature_moments.moment: A mutating virus turns the vaccine into a fortification the virus can learn to evade.
signature_moments.source_phrase: you're training the virus to be much more evasive
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the compact logic of his vaccine critique.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The idea that scientific authority can become unquestionable truth rather than debate and exploration.
Jiang's term for tycoons he says represented deeper financial power rather than self-made wealth.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang refers back to COVID-era vaccine politics from a 2026 interview vantage.
